agent2 (Round 2 summarized): In obstetrics, a "high angle case" refers to abnormal fetal head positioning relative to the pelvic inlet, assessed using the fetal mento-parietal angle (FMP). Although an exact threshold isn’t specified in available data, FMP > 35° is clinically recognized as indicating potential cephalopelvic disproportion and complicating labor.  

(Note: This summary focuses only on core clinical definitions related to fetal presentation.)  
\end{document}. Answer choice: C
